pending_tickets.thesis: APPROVED 5:11pm - queued for the open. Redeploy the freed SOFI cash into the confirmed leader FNGU (3x FANG+) - the day's ONLY green long mover (+3.1%). Pre-arm buy-stop at $30.20 so it fires at the open only if the tape confirms strength after tonight's TSMC print (2am ET; beat+raise is the base case, 8 straight beats). Completes the Playbook de-camp->leader swing. 6 sh x $30.60 marketable limit = $183.6 reserve < $198.99 BP.

coverage.name: ProShares UltraPro QQQ (3x)
coverage.thesis: **The 3x-Nasdaq core - held into TSMC.** 10 sh @ $76.29, settled $74.35 (-0.9% today, -2.4% vs entry). Faded with the sell-the-news chip tape but reclaimed off the $72.09 intraday low; ~$6 above the $68.50 GTC stop.
coverage.hold_reason: Our 3x-Nasdaq core - 10 shares from $76.29 on 7/9, the high-conviction bet the AI/tech uptrend keeps compounding. Down today because tech faded on the chip sell-the-news, but the $68.50 GTC stop bounds it and ASML's beat-and-raise says the capex thesis is intact. Holding through tonight's TSMC print; a clean stop break or a soft TSMC reaction would make us cut and rotate.

projection.basis: 3x Nasdaq core; tracks a green post-TSMC tape

coverage.name: SoFi Technologies
coverage.thesis: **The flat laggard, now the standing rotation candidate.** 10 sh @ $18.76, now $17.96 (-3.2% today, -4.3% vs entry). No catalyst; it's the capital the book wants concentrated in the leader. The de-camp jammed mechanically all day (resting stop reserves the shares) and is retired to a clean re-run after TSMC. Protected by the $17.60 GTC stop.
coverage.hold_reason: We SOLD this today at $17.94 (3:35pm) - it was the flat laggard from 7/10 tying up ~$180 of dead capital, and freeing it broke a multi-day cash-lock. No catalyst here, so it's back to watch-only. The freed cash redeploys into the strongest confirmed mover after TSMC.

projection.basis: exited today; no catalyst, watch for re-entry

coverage.name: GraniteShares 2x NVDA
coverage.thesis: **2x NVDA - held, well above the stop.** 1 sh live @ $30.59, settled $33.38 (+9.1% from entry, +0.6% today). Held green through the entire chip unwind on NVDA's relative strength; the $31.30 GTC stop trails the gain.
coverage.hold_reason: A single 2x-NVDA share from $30.59 - small, but our cleanest live long on the AI leader. NVDA clawed back to +0.3% off a -3% intraday low on its own catalysts (Kawasaki AI-shipyard deal, limited H200 China shipments) - exactly the relative strength we want into TSMC. Holding; the $31.30 stop locks in a profit.

coverage.name: MicroSectors FANG+ (3x)
coverage.thesis: **The day's ONLY green leader - and the staged live redeploy.** Green all day (+3.1%) while the chip/memory complex was flushed - relative strength, not a knife. Paper holds 520 sh @ $28.46 (now $29.80, +$697), stop trailed to $29. The freed ~$199 SOFI cash is pre-armed to buy 6 sh on a $30.20 breakout at the open (confirms a strong, TSMC-validated open before buying).
coverage.hold_reason: 3x FANG+ (mega-cap tech) - paper's biggest winner (520 sh, +$697) and the day's best long mover, green all day while chips unwound. It's the live redeploy target for the freed SOFI cash, staged rather than chased at today's HOD into the binary TSMC print. Pre-armed as a $30.20 buy-stop so it only fires if the open confirms strength; a $25.30 GTC stop bounds the 3x downside.
coverage.size_note: Staged redeploy for the freed ~$199 - pre-arm buy-stop trigger $30.20, fires at the open only if TSMC's reaction confirms the tape (not chased at today's HOD).

projection.basis: approved swing; continuation on TSMC beat+raise

coverage.name: Direxion 3x Semis
coverage.thesis: **The wreck we dodged live - now a paper reversal test.** Gapped +4% on ASML (~$184), flushed to -14% ($150), reclaimed to $165. Paper holds a 10-sh starter @ $163.63 (+0.7%), stop $148 (below the washout low), testing 'buy the reclaim off a higher low' into TSMC tomorrow. Still un-owned live.
coverage.hold_reason: 3x semis - the epicenter of today's sell-the-news dump (-8%, off a +4% open top). Small paper starter (10 sh) bought as a washout-reversal bet into TSMC tomorrow; it's the highest-beta snap-back if the print confirms capex, and the $148 GTC stop bounds the binary downside. Owned NONE in live, which dodged the whole flush.

projection.basis: oversold 3x-semi; biggest bounce IF TSMC beats (binary)

coverage.name: Constellation Energy
coverage.thesis: **Power-for-AI relative strength.** 16 sh paper @ $260.68, now $257.30 (+0.3% today). Green-to-flat while chips bled. Paper stop $236.
coverage.hold_reason: Our AI-power/utility play - the electricity behind the data centers - and a deliberate non-semi diversifier. Green-to-flat on a red chip day, doing its job. Watching $255-262; a break below would be our tell to reassess.

coverage.name: NVIDIA
coverage.thesis: **Closed GREEN off its $204 low - the resilient-chip tell.** Settled $212.5 (+0.3%) and firmed to $212.4 after-hours after clawing back from the $203.84 intraday low as the tape reversed. Paper holds 130 sh @ $210.26 (+$178) + 8x Aug $220 calls now -1.3% ($7.70 vs $7.80) - held as convexity into TSMC.
coverage.hold_reason: The core AI name - paper holds shares plus an Aug 220 call for convexity. Closed green (+0.3%) after clawing back from its $204 intraday low, and JPMorgan/Jensen/Son all frame the chip dump as a positioning unwind, not an AI-cycle top, so we hold for the rebound + tonight's TSMC print. A decisive break of $200 breaks the thesis and we cut the call.

projection.basis: cleanest TSMC beneficiary; recovered off lows

coverage.name: Palantir
coverage.thesis: **Green-flat software, holding the reclaim.** 50 sh paper @ $135.22, now $133.97 (flat today). Software > semis in this unwind; holding above yesterday's $122.7 spike-low reclaim.
coverage.hold_reason: AI-software leader we bought on a breakout reclaim; a non-semi name holding flat while hardware bled. Software keeps outperforming chips in this unwind. Paper stop $121.50 protects the swing.

projection.basis: reclaimed a huge intraday range; software bid holds

coverage.name: ProShares UltraPro S&P500 (3x)
coverage.thesis: **3x broad-market ballast - the tell.** 95 sh paper @ $145.38, now $145.42 (+0.7% today). Broad S&P GREEN while the Nasdaq/chip side bled - proof this is a sector unwind, not a market break.
coverage.hold_reason: Our 3x broad-S&P position - deliberately less tech-concentrated than TQQQ, and the accountability's named 'less-fragile core.' Green, doing its ballast job on a chip-heavy red day. Paper stop $124; we hold unless the broad tape rolls over with the Nasdaq.

projection.basis: 3x S&P; broad-tape proxy on a green TSMC read

coverage.name: Direxion 3x Inverse Semis
coverage.thesis: **Biggest watchlist mover - un-ownable live, now fading.** 3x-inverse semis ~+18% on the chip unwind (recently reverse-split, vendor % distorted), but rolling over as the semi reclaim builds (SOXL $150→$165). Live can't hold inverse leverage cleanly; capture only exists on paper.

projection.basis: inverse-semi; only wins if TSMC misses

coverage.name: Bloom Energy
coverage.thesis: **Spiked to $244 then dumped with the spec cohort.** ~$234.6 (-3.6%), bouncing with the tape off the $217.90 flush. No position; watching a real floor to reclaim.

projection.basis: fuel cells; snap-back off the flush

coverage.name: IonQ
coverage.thesis: **Quantum bleeding, low-quality bounce.** ~$37.8 (-3.9%), lifting with the reclaim but no lead. High-beta spec is a poor place to concentrate in a de-risking tape.

projection.basis: quantum; oversold relief only if risk-on returns

coverage.name: Rigetti Computing
coverage.thesis: **Worst of the spec cohort.** ~$15.4 (-4.6%). Deepest high-beta bleed; a bounce here is the lowest-quality on the board. Stay out.

coverage.name: Oklo
coverage.thesis: **Nuclear-for-AI, holding better than the spec cohort.** ~$45.7 (-1.0%). The power-for-AI theme we like, but CEG is the cleaner, held expression.

coverage.name: Vertiv
coverage.thesis: **Data-center infra reclaimed from $290.** ~$302.4 (-0.4%), bounced with the tape off the $289.87 low. Good long-term AI-infra name but no clean entry yet.

feed.text: 8:40pm overnight monitor: nothing traded since 8:12pm; both books protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC, none naked), FNGU pre-arm resting for the 7/16 open. Confirmed TSMC reports 2am ET Thursday - June rev +68% YoY keeps a beat+raise the base case that trips the $30.20 buy-stop on a strong open.

8:12pm evening monitor: after-hours closed, no change. Both books protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC stops, none naked). FNGU pre-arm approved 5:11pm, queued to rest a $30.20 buy-stop at the open pending TSMC's pre-market print. SOFI exit reconciled (flat, ~$199 freed).

7:40pm evening monitor: both books re-verified protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC stops, none naked); the FNGU pre-arm ($30.20 buy-stop, $25.30 GTC stop, ~$184 reserve < $199 BP) is resting for the 7/16 open. TSMC prelim showed Q2 rev +36% YoY, AI 61% of sales - a beat+raise base case into the 2am print; no chase into a binary after-hours tape.

7:10pm evening monitor: both books re-verified protected (2 live + 8 paper GTC stops, none naked), FNGU pre-arm resting for the 7/16 open. TSMC prints 2am ET - beat+raise base case; NVDA firmed green after-hours as the resilient-chip tell.
Evening monitor: both books protected, FNGU pre-arm resting for the 7/16 open pending TSMC's 2am print.

Adam approved the FNGU redeploy at 5:11pm - the fast-lane now holds it and places the $30.20 pre-arm buy-stop at tomorrow's open. Fires only if FNGU breaks today's $29.87 high after TSMC; a weak open never trips it and the ~$199 cash is preserved. Completes the SOFI de-camp -> confirmed-leader swing.

5:42pm re-verify: nothing moved after-hours, both books fully protected into TSMC's 2am print. Preview confirmed - ~$39.3B Q2 revenue (+30% YoY), 65.5-67.5% gross margin, 8 straight beats; a beat+raise extends the mega-cap-tech leadership FNGU rides.

After-hours check (4:40pm): re-verified both books are protected into TSMC's 2am print - 2/2 live stops (NVDL $31.30, TQQQ $68.50) + 8/8 paper GTC stops live, no naked position. FNGU redeploy stays pre-armed for the open ($30.20 buy-stop, $25.30 stop, ~$184 reserve < $199 BP). Sitting tight - extended hours is limit-only and TSMC is binary, so no off-hours chase.

4:10pm close - final grade C-. Red -1.1% while FNGU (best long mover, +3.1%) ran and we held zero live = ~0% long capture. Credits banked: dodged the -6% SOXL / -5% quantum wreck, held all stops, and finally broke the cash-lock (SOFI out @ $17.94, ~$199 freed). FNGU redeploy staged for the open, gated on tonight's TSMC print.

Data-integrity fix: 7/14's after-hours $997.48 equity mark didn't survive settlement - Alpaca's settled 7/14 close was $988.46. Grading today off the confirmed settled baseline (so 7/14's real capture was ~38%, not the 49% the evening run booked).

The SOFI de-camp finally cleared at 3:35pm - after no-op'ing all day on the resting stop, the fast-lane cancelled the $17.60 GTC stop and sold all 10 shares @ $17.94, freeing ~$199. Breaks the multi-day cash-lock. Redeploy is staged for after tomorrow's TSMC print - not chasing FNGU at its high into a binary catalyst.

3:45pm - the freed ~$199 is dry powder, not lazy cash: 80% of the book is still working (TQQQ + NVDL) and the redeploy fills at tomorrow's open either way, so I'm waiting for TSMC (pre-market) to confirm the strongest mover rather than buying FNGU's HOD in the last 15 min. All stops verified live + paper; nothing naked; 0 day-trades used.

Retired the SOFI->FNGU de-camp for today. It never filled - the resting $17.60 GTC stop reserves the 10 shares, so the close no-ops (fast-lane cancel-stop-before-close bug, still unfixed). And it's now too late to dump SOFI at its ~$17.90 low to chase FNGU after it already ran +2.6% into the close - that's bad execution, not a rotation. Holding SOFI protected; re-running the swap clean after TSMC prints tomorrow AM.

3:14pm - book -1.7% on the wrong side of a sell-the-news chip unwind (heavy TQQQ, zero FNGU live). All stops verified, nothing naked; PDT budget full (0 day-trades used). No new live ticket this run - no clean fundable fire-now setup that beats holding into tomorrow's binary TSMC print, and cash is locked at $19.59. Flagged the fast-lane stuck-close bug to fix before tomorrow's re-run.
Trailed the FNGU paper winner's stop $28.80 → $29.00 on today's higher low - locks ~+$280 above the $28.46 cost while the day's leader (+3.2%) keeps leading.

2:16pm - the reclaim is holding (SPY +0.4%, QQQ 718, SMH off its -3.7% low; FNGU leads +3.2%). Kept the live SOFI→FNGU de-camp ticket resting for Adam's tap - the right move, and I won't stack a second ticket into the same $19.59 cash. Own none of the -6.9% SOXL / -3.6% BE / -4.6% RGTI wreck; all stops verified. Also corrected an inflated since-inception alpha: on a consistent 7/2-close anchor we honestly trail buy-and-hold TQQQ (+1.6%) with the book -2.2% - the de-camp is the cure.

Live de-camp ticket sent (approve-to-fill): sell the flat SOFI laggard to free ~$200 into the leader FNGU. Multi-day-hold exit = PDT-free. The fast-lane now cancels the resting $17.60 stop before closing, so this should finally execute. [RETIRED 3:14pm - never filled: the resting $17.60 stop reserves the 10 shares so close_position no-ops (fast-lane cancel-stop bug). Re-running clean after TSMC tomorrow.]
Paper lab bought a SOXL washout-reversal starter - it reclaimed $150→$161 off the flush and TSMC reports tomorrow. $148 GTC stop below the washout low. Testing 'buy the reclaim off a higher low.'
Trailed the FNGU paper winner's stop $28.30 → $28.80 on today's higher low - locks the gain above cost while the leader keeps leading (+2.4%).

1:52pm - the memory-chip wreck is REVERSING off the lows (SOXL $150→$161, TQQQ $72→$74, QQQ 710→716) with the broad market green (SPY +0.2%, UPRO +0.4%). This is the reclaim I was waiting for, so I'm finally executing the standing de-camp: live SOFI→FNGU ticket sent, paper testing the SOXL reversal + trailed the FNGU winner. Still red on the day (-1.7%) and behind the FNGU leader (+2.4%) - poor long capture - but I'm ACTING on the cash-lock fix, not camping. All live + paper stops verified.

1:15pm - held both books; the memory-chip bear market is STABILIZING off the midday flush (SOXL -14%->-10.6%, QQQ 710->714, book -3.8%->-2.4%) but the reclaim is UNCONFIRMED - QQQ still under its ~720 20/50-day and SMH -3% is not green. Per my earned rule I did NOT chase the bounce (buy the RECLAIM, not the knife); the SOFI->leader rotation stays armed for QQQ>720 + semis green. Broad market is fine (SPY +0.0%, UPRO -0.3%), and we own NONE of the -10.6% SOXL / -7.2% BE / -5.9% RGTI wreck. All 3 live + 7 paper GTC stops verified live; no naked positions.

12:40pm - chip unwind deepened again (SOXL -13.4%->-14.3%, TQQQ -2.8%->-3.8%, book now -3.8%). QQQ 711 still under its 20d/50d, tape making fresh lows with no base. Held both books, all stops verified live, no naked positions. No knife-catch, no chase - the next long shot is a semi reclaim (higher low + level reclaim), not this.

Reconciled Adam's 10:33am SOFI-close tap: it did NOT change the book - the resting $17.60 GTC stop reserves all 10 shares, so the close no-op'd (no fill, cash still $19.59). Since SOXL - the rotation's target - then cratered to -14%, I'm deliberately not retrying the exit at SOFI's intraday low. SOFI stays held and protected by the $17.60 stop.

12:15pm - chip unwind DEEPENED (SOXL -13.4%, BE -8.3%, IONQ -5.9%, SMH -3.7%). QQQ 713 under its 20d & 50d = weak regime, so no 3x chase. Held the well-stopped live book (-2.7%, all above stops); owning none of the wreck makes the shelved SOFI->SOXL dodge worth ~$31 now. Rotation stays ARMED for a green reclaim only.
Trailed FNGU paper stop $26.50 -> $28.30 on today's higher low - the book's lone winner (+$603) is now locked to house money.

11:47am - chip unwind deepened (SOXL -8.7%, SMH -2.5%, IONQ -4.5%, RGTI -5.2%) and QQQ faded to 716, back under its 718 50-day. My rule: only rotate SOFI->leader on a green reclaim - not met, so I held and did NOT chase FNGU (+2.9%). Book -1.4% but owns none of the wreck. Trailed the FNGU paper stop up to $26.50.
Trailed FNGU paper stop up $24.30 -> $26.50 on a higher low - protecting the book's one big winner (+$650) while chips bleed.

11:16am - held both books, no changes. The semi unwind is a rotation, not a crash: chips bleed (SOXL -9%, SMH -3%, BE -4.5%) while mega-cap + power run green (FNGU +2.1%, CEG +1.8%). QQQ under its 50-day = weak regime, so no 3x chase. Kept the SOFI de-camp shelved - the SOXL target faded -9%. Correction: the fast-lane close is NOT buggy (it already cancels the resting stop first) - the de-camp never fired because the target faded, not because of a code bug. All 3 live stops verified.

10:48am - GREEN->RED reversal + a real find. The 10:17 rally flipped: semis got hit (SOXL -9.5%, BE -4.9%) on a crowded-position unwind (JPMorgan: not an AI-cycle top), while broad/mega-cap held green (FNGU +2.4%, UPRO +1.0%, CEG +2.2%). QQQ back under its 50-day = weak regime, so I did NOT chase 3x FNGU into the selloff. Bigger: the approved SOFI sell can't fire because the fast-lane 'close' doesn't cancel the resting stop first - the actual reason 5 runs of 'sell SOFI' never executed. Held both books, all stops verified.

SOFI sell was approved but correctly never executed - the ASML rotation target (SOXL) gapped +4% then reversed to -9%, so cutting stable SOFI to chase a broken tape was the wrong trade. (Correction: NOT a fast-lane 'close' bug - the code does cancel the resting stop first; the trade just stopped making sense.) Holding SOFI - today's relative outperformer - still stopped at $17.60.
Rotated paper into CEG - AI-power name breaking $236->$262, a clean catalyst instead of a decaying 3x-semi. $236 GTC stop on.
Cut the 3x-semi SOXL - chopping $165-192 with no clean uptrend, the leverage-decay trap. Took the -$241, redeployed to CEG.

9:45am - SELL-THE-NEWS fade. ASML's beat gapped semis up then they dumped: SOXL +4% open -> -1% at its low, the worst name on the board. Retired the SOFI->SOXL chase instead of catching the knife (the ticket's own premortem said don't buy SOXL into a broken tape - and it broke). Held the book: TQQQ is green +0.7%, the relative-strength winner. Hunting a semi reclaim or a fresh leader.

9:10am - chip bid firmed into the open after a SOFT June PPI (wholesale inflation fell): SOXL +4.2% (~$184) now leads (was +2.9%), TQQQ +2.0%. SOFI->SOXL rotation still GO for the bell (approve-to-fill, PDT-free). All 3 live stops verified; paper's 25-sh SOXL add resting for the open.

8:40am - PPI + Morgan Stanley both cleared without a risk-off shock (MS record Q2, tape held, SOXL still +2.9%). SOFI->SOXL rotation is GO for the open (approve-to-fill, PDT-free multi-day exit). All 3 live stops verified; paper's 25-sh SOXL add (order 8c95) confirmed resting for the open.

8:15am - final pre-PPI check, plan intact. SOXL +3.0% (~$182) still leads but the pop is cooling into the 8:30 print; all 3 live stops verified (NVDL $31.30 / SOFI $17.60 / TQQQ $68.50). SOFI->SOXL rotation staged approve-to-fill; paper's SOXL add (25 sh) is queued for the open. Taking the PPI + MS reaction before redeploying live.

7:40am - holding the line into the 8:30 data. SOXL +4.4% (~$184) still leads, ASML confirmed +3.8% pre-market on its raised 2026 guide. PPI + Morgan Stanley both drop 8:30 - the last read before the open. SOFI->SOXL rotation stays staged approve-to-fill; I redeploy at the open after seeing PPI's chip reaction. All 3 GTC stops verified ($68.50/$17.60/$31.30).

7:15am - fresh chip headline, plan unchanged. US approved LIMITED NVDA H200 exports to China (KeyBanc PT->$330) and NVDA popped ~+4%, but a US official called the volumes 'trivial' and the pop faded to flat (NVDA ~$211.8, NVDL ~$33.15). SOXL (+4.2%, ~$184) is still the clean pre-market leader, so the SOFI->SOXL rotation holds: flat SOFI sells at the open (ticket approve-to-fill), SOXL goes on right after. Both books reconcile, all 9 GTC stops live. No new order - BP $19.59 until SOFI frees ~$205.

6:45am - pared-pop recheck on FRESH SIP pre-market (not overnight marks): SOXL +3.8% (~$183.4, off the +6.1% overnight high) still leads; TQQQ ~$75.9 is flat vs its settled $75.79 close; ASML settled ~+4.4% after a +7% open spike but chips stay broadly green (SMH +1.2%, Intel/Lam +3%). Plan unchanged - flat SOFI sells at the open, SOXL goes on right after, and buying it near +3.8% is a cleaner entry than the spike. All 9 GTC stops live, both books reconcile.

6:10am recheck: futures now confirm the overnight read - Nasdaq-100 +0.4% led by chips (ASML +7%), not a fade. Both books reconcile to the broker, all 9 GTC stops live. Plan unchanged - flat SOFI sells at the open, SOXL goes on right after.

latest_recap: Wed 7/15 ~4:10pm ET post-close finalize (market closed). FINDINGS: (1) Final grade C- - live settled -1.1% ($977.17 vs Alpaca's confirmed settled 7/14 $988.46) while the day's best long mover FNGU ran +3.1% = ~0% long capture; a red day on the wrong side (heavy 3x-Nasdaq TQQQ into a faded, chip-sell-the-news tape). (2) Genuine credits: owned NONE of the -6.3% SOXL / -5.2% RGTI / -4.6% IONQ / -3.6% SOFI wreck; held every stop (2/2 live, 8/8 paper), no naked position; finally broke the multi-day cash-lock by exiting SOFI @ $17.94 (freed ~$199); did NOT chase FNGU at its HOD into tonight's binary TSMC print. (3) Data-integrity finding: 7/14's after-hours $997.48 equity mark did NOT survive settlement - Alpaca reports settled 7/14 = $988.46 (balance_asof), so 7/14's real capture was ~38% (not the 49% the evening run booked). Durable fix: grade off settled balance_asof, treat same-evening equity as provisional; corrected the live curve (7/14 -> $988.46) and score (bestDay +3.2%, winRate 50% on 2 closed round-trips: NVDA frac +$0.75 win, SOFI -$8.17 loss). (4) PDT budget fully intact - 0 same-day round-trips today (SOFI was a 5-day hold). (5) Staged a FNGU pre-arm ticket (2026-07-16-1): 6 sh, buy-stop trigger $30.20 (confirms a TSMC-validated strong open before buying), $25.30 GTC stop - the SOFI->leader redeploy, gap-handled for the open. TSMC reports 2am ET; beat+raise is the base case.

activity.title: Wed 7/15 ~3:14pm - market OPEN (~46 min to close). Verified all stops (3 live + 8 paper, none naked). Retired the mechanically-jammed SOFI->FNGU de-camp ticket (2026-07-15-3) for today; re-running clean after TSMC tomorrow. Book -1.7% = poor long capture on the sell-the-news chip unwind; dodged the -8% SOXL wreck. Paper held (99% deployed into TSMC). No live ticket surfaced.

Wed 7/15 ~2:40pm - market OPEN (~80 min to close). Verify-and-hold both books; nothing material changed since 2:16. Fresh pull: get_clock + LIVE account/positions/orders + PAPER account/positions/orders + snapshot(12-name universe) + get_market_movers + TQQQ 16d bars + web-search. Reconciled clean both ways (LIVE == broker: TQQQ 10 / SOFI 10 / NVDL 1; PAPER == broker: CEG 16 / FNGU 520 / NVDA 130 / 8 NVDA Aug-$220 calls / NVDL 80 / PLTR 50 / SOXL 10 / TQQQ 282 / UPRO 95). All 3 live + 8 paper equity GTC stops re-verified live (LIVE $68.50/$17.60/$31.30; PAPER FNGU $29.00 / SOXL $148 / CEG $236 / NVDL $31.30 / PLTR $121.50 / TQQQ $68.50 / UPRO $124 / NVDA $186); no naked positions. No new queue fills since -10-3; ticket 2026-07-15-3 (SELL SOFI) still resting un-tapped (the earlier 15-2 'close' no-op'd, SOFI still held + $17.60 stop intact). LIVE equity $975.86 (-1.3% today), cash $19.59; PAPER $98,317 (-0.8% today, FNGU +$445 today). Tape: memory selloff (SK Hynix -10.7%, Micron -7.3%, AMD -6.4%) with money rotating INTO mega-caps (AMZN/MSFT/GOOGL +3%, AAPL +4%) - FNGU +3.0% leads, TQQQ -1.0% lags. No live trade (BP $19.59 funds nothing until SOFI frees ~$180); no paper churn into a choppy tape 80 min from close; no stop trails (live holds under entry on a red day; NVDL stop already just under today's low). Grade C intraday, poor long capture, dodged the wreck. Benchmark TQQQ +1.3% since 7/2 close, alpha -3.7. Day-trade budget 3/3. TSMC Thu 7/16. Published engine-data.json only (no data.json, no git). Backup engine-data.backup-2026-07-15-1440.json.

Wed 7/15 ~1:52pm - market OPEN. Afternoon reclaim off the midday lows (SOXL $150→$161, QQQ 710→716, broad market green).
